situation.headline: US is conducting sustained nightly strikes against Iran for at least 10 consecutive nights while Iran retaliates against US bases in Kuwait, strikes Gulf Arab infrastructure, and activates Houthi blockade of Saudi ports — a de facto regional war is underway

domains.name: Middle East
domains.label: regional escalation 90d
domains.status: Active US-Iran kinetic exchange ongoing with 17 US KIA, Iranian strikes on Kuwait infrastructure and US forces at Camp Arifjan, Houthi blockade of Saudi ports expanding, and Strait of Hormuz tanker attacks threatening to sever Gulf energy exports

domains.name: Indo-Pacific
domains.label: China coercion escalation
domains.status: US military resources and attention are heavily committed to the Middle East theater, creating a window of opportunity for Chinese coercion that Beijing is unlikely to ignore; no direct indicators yet but opportunistic risk is elevated

domains.name: Europe
domains.label: NATO cohesion fracture
domains.status: Spain airbrushing Trump from a victory photo signals deepening transatlantic political friction, while US assets are stretched thin by Middle East operations and Ukraine front remains active with drone strikes on Russian airbases

domains.name: Energy
domains.label: supply shock 60d
domains.status: Gas already back above four dollars, Strait of Hormuz tanker attacks ongoing with one vessel crew abandoning ship, Houthi blockade of Saudi ports announced, and Kuwaiti desalination infrastructure damaged — a multi-vector supply disruption is converging

domains.name: US Politics
domains.label: House flip Nov 2026
domains.status: Kalshi markets show Todd Blanche surging to 89 percent for next AG suggesting significant internal political churn, electricity prices up 18 percent versus Trump's promise of cuts, and wartime casualties will become a domestic political liability if conflict drags

situation.unseen: Iran's simultaneous targeting of Kuwait's power and desalination infrastructure is a strategic signal directed at Gulf Arab states warning them not to facilitate US basing rights or overflight — this is coercive deterrence aimed at fracturing the Gulf Cooperation Council coalition underpinning US regional posture, and if Kuwait or UAE balk at hosting US operations the entire strike campaign loses its forward logistics foundation
situation.criticalWindow: The next 10 to 21 days are decisive: US intelligence assesses strikes are not moving Iran's negotiating position, 17 US KIA with more expected, Pentagon budget reportedly exhausted, and Iran is escalating rather than deterring — the administration faces a binary choice between a negotiated off-ramp that requires significant concessions or a qualitative escalation targeting Iranian regime leadership or nuclear sites, and delayed decision-making increases the probability of catastrophic miscalculation in the Strait of Hormuz
watchFor: Strait of Hormuz closure or sustained tanker interdiction forcing Lloyd's war-risk insurance suspension and triggering oil price spike above 120 dollars per barrel
Iranian strike causing mass US casualties at Camp Arifjan or Al Udeid forcing congressional war authorization debate and constraining presidential options
Kuwait or Bahrain announcing suspension of US basing rights under Iranian pressure, collapsing forward strike posture
Chinese naval activity near Taiwan or in the South China Sea exploiting US carrier group commitment to the Gulf theater
situation.confidenceNote: The OSINT feed is high-tempo and largely unverified tactical reporting; casualty figures, strike damage assessments, and Iranian command-and-control status are all subject to significant fog of war distortion; the prediction markets contain almost no Middle East conflict instruments making quantitative calibration of escalation risk impossible from this context alone; domestic US political impacts of a protracted air campaign with ongoing casualties are highly uncertain and depend heavily on media framing and congressional reaction not visible in this feed
situation.topPattern: The US is executing a sustained air campaign against Iran that its own intelligence community assesses will not achieve its deterrence objective, while simultaneously suffering personnel losses, watching Gulf Arab infrastructure crumble under Iranian retaliation, absorbing a Houthi blockade of Saudi ports, and running out of Pentagon funding — Kalshi's 89-cent surge on Todd Blanche as next AG suggests internal political turbulence that may further destabilize strategic coherence precisely when unified decision-making is most critical

signals.headline: US-Iran strikes cycle escalates; Kuwait infrastructure hit; shipping corridor under sustained attack
signals.summary: Ten consecutive nights of US strikes on Iranian military targets; Iran responded with ballistic missiles targeting US Camp Arifjan in Kuwait; Iranian drones/missiles hit Kuwaiti power plants and desalination (4th attack); tanker struck in Strait of Hormuz with crew abandonment reported. Red Sea blockade threat now live on prediction markets.
signals.signal: Escalation cycle is locked in—neither side backing down. US intelligence assesses strikes unlikely to shift Iranian negotiating position, meaning this becomes protracted attrition. Kuwait infrastructure damage forces US logistical exposure; maritime closure in Hormuz/Red Sea threatens global energy supply and drives oil prices upward ($4+ gasoline already emerging in headlines). Regional proxy networks (Houthis, IRGC) now executing coordinated multi-theater strikes.
signals.contradicts: Trump administration implied Iran deterrence via strength; intelligence community now explicitly denies strikes will produce diplomatic leverage.

signals.headline: Trump administration promises on electricity costs failing within first term; rates up 18% vs promised 50% cut
signals.summary: Campaign pledge to cut electricity prices in half within 18 months; actual data shows 18% rate increase. Inflation-sensitive issue that drives voter confidence in economic competence.
signals.signal: Early credibility damage on flagship economic promise. Markets already repricing Trump policy efficacy—bond traders 'tuning out Trump posts' per WSJ. If energy price trajectory accelerates before 2028 election cycle, opens Democratic vulnerability messaging on cost-of-living and validates 'all talk' narrative.
signals.contradicts: Trump's core pitch that executive efficiency delivers immediate household relief.

signals.headline: Todd Blanche Attorney General odds surge to 89%; Lee Zeldin AG odds collapse from 8% to 5%
signals.summary: Prediction market signal showing 7-cent move in 24h toward Blanche; Zeldin simultaneous 3-cent fade. AG selection signals approach to Justice Dept independence and political prosecution risk.
signals.signal: Market confidence shifting toward Blanche (Trump's personal lawyer, directly embedded in legal defense strategy) over Zeldin (externally credible former congressmen). This suggests market pricing in higher risk of DOJ subordination to Trump personal interests and lower risk of institutional guardrails. Implies prediction market traders expect more aggressive political use of federal law enforcement.
signals.contradicts: Public messaging that Trump cabinet appointments represent institutional stability and professionalism.

signals.headline: Iran Foreign Minister confirms never met new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei; isolation signals persist
signals.summary: Abbas Araghchi on record stating he has had no direct contact with successor Supreme Leader Mojtaba, only a 'very small number' have met him. Follows succession/health concerns around Khamenei.
signals.signal: Leadership transition opacity and information compartmentalization suggest either contested succession legitimacy or deliberate OPSEC during escalation cycle. Reduces predictability of Iranian decision-making—new leader's strategic preferences unknown even to senior foreign ministry. Increases risk of miscalculation in escalation de-escalation calculations.
signals.contradicts: Assumption of stable, centralized Iranian command authority during military operations.

signals.headline: 2028 Democratic primary markets show fragmentation; Newsom 19%, Ossoff 15%, AOC 15%, Whitmer 2%
signals.summary: No Democratic frontrunner emerging in prediction markets; top candidate under 20%. Multiple regional/generational bids splitting vote (centrist Newsom vs progressive Ossoff/AOC). Whitmer momentum stalled despite recent national profile.
signals.signal: Democratic party structure lacks clear successor to Biden; prediction market pricing reflects genuine internal uncertainty about 2028 nominee. Wide-open field increases insurgent/outsider odds and suggests establishment still consolidating after 2024 cycle. Republicans show tighter clustering (Vance 40%, Rubio 30%), implying smoother GOP succession planning.
signals.contradicts: Any narrative of incumbent-party momentum or settled primary structure.

signals.headline: Pentagon out of money; Red Sea blockade and Iranian capacity destruction converging on global LNG/energy supply
signals.summary: Pentagon budgetary crisis + sustained Houthi Red Sea blockade threat + Iranian infrastructure strikes creating compounding supply-chain stress on energy. Europe facing 120°F temperatures for first time.
signals.signal: US fiscal constraint collides with energy security crisis. If Pentagon cannot sustain military ops in Middle East AND energy prices spike due to shipping closure, stagflation risk in US economy accelerates. Commodity/energy traders pricing in sustained supply disruption; oil $4+ gasoline already visible. Defense industrial base cannot be maintained if budget exhausted during active regional conflict.
signals.contradicts: Assumption that US can simultaneously manage large-scale Middle East operations while maintaining NATO/Taiwan deterrence posture.

scanNote: Context reflects active kinetic conflict phase (Iran-US strikes ongoing), cascading geopolitical failures on Trump economic promises, and market-visible uncertainty on both Middle East escalation trajectory and 2028 Democratic structure; energy/fiscal constraints now binding constraints on US strategic flexibility.

markets.label: Netanyahu arrested before Oct 1 2026
markets.rationale: Active US-Iran kinetic conflict and regional focus on Iran/Houthi threats dominates the Middle East picture; no credible enforcement mechanism for ICC warrant has emerged, and market spiked 4 cents on low OI suggesting thin noise trade rather than informed signal.

markets.label: Netanyahu arrested before Nov 1 2026
markets.rationale: Same structural logic as the October contract but with an extra month of window; the 7-cent single-day spike on very low OI is a thin-market artifact, not credible intelligence-driven pricing.

markets.label: Netanyahu arrested before Jan 1 2027
markets.rationale: Extended window marginally improves odds, but ongoing US-Israel operational alignment against Iran makes any allied pressure for ICC enforcement politically untenable through end of 2026; 11-cent spike on tiny OI is noise.

markets.label: No US acquisition of Greenland this term
markets.rationale: US military and financial bandwidth is heavily consumed by active Iran conflict, Red Sea blockade, and Pentagon budget stress, making a Greenland acquisition deal within this term highly unlikely; market at 81 percent is well-calibrated.

markets.label: US buys Greenland before 2027
markets.rationale: Pentagon funding shortfalls and active multi-theater kinetic operations make a Greenland transaction before 2027 effectively impossible regardless of rhetorical intent; 6 percent market is slightly generous.

markets.label: Todd Blanche becomes next AG
markets.rationale: The 7-cent single-day jump to 89 percent on meaningful volume amid ongoing national security and DOJ pressure context suggests well-informed positioning; no competing candidate in the data generates comparable momentum.

markets.label: Gavin Newsom Dem nominee 2028
markets.rationale: Highest-volume Democratic nomination market with a 1-cent decline today; ongoing energy price pain and electricity rate headlines hurt California-branded candidates, and the Dem field remains deeply fragmented per overall market distribution.

markets.label: JD Vance Republican nominee 2028
markets.rationale: As sitting VP during an active military conflict with Iran and multiple domestic crises, Vance is the structural frontrunner for the GOP nomination; 40 percent market price is reasonable and stable.

markets.label: Marco Rubio Republican nominee 2028
markets.rationale: Rubio's foreign policy role during the Iran conflict elevates his profile, but Vance's incumbency advantage as VP structurally limits Rubio's ceiling; 30 percent market appears slightly overpriced relative to that dynamic.

markets.label: US pays $100B-$299B for Greenland
markets.rationale: A 5-cent spike on low volume is likely speculative noise; active kinetic commitments in the Middle East and a Pentagon budget crisis make any near-term Greenland purchase at any price implausible, let alone a negotiated nine-figure deal.

watchSignals: Escalation pace of US-Iran strike cycle: CENTCOM conducted tenth consecutive night of strikes as of Jul 20; watch for Iranian response timing and targeting selectivity (Kuwait infrastructure vs US bases) over next 72-96 hours

Strait of Hormuz closure probability: UKMTO reports multiple vessel strikes and crew abandonments; Polymarket Bab el-Mandel closure odds rising; monitor oil tanker attack frequency and insurance/shipping diversion patterns
Houthi blockade declaration enforcement: Iranian-backed group announced Saudi port targeting; assess actual interdiction capability vs rhetorical posture and impact on Red Sea commerce flow
Todd Blanche Attorney General confirmation: Kalshi AG market shows Blanche at 89% (+7¢ in 24h); watch Senate Judiciary hearings and any late opposition moves that could shift market pricing

US military readiness signals: Pentagon "out of money" headline + Hegseth fitness criticism + Delaware dignified transfer scheduling (now Wednesday) suggest force posture strain; monitor CENTCOM logistics and personnel casualty updates

Iranian negotiating position hardening: Intelligence assessment says strikes unlikely to soften Tehran's stance; watch for Iranian military response rhetoric, new drone/missile deployments, or proxy activation in Iraq/Syria that signal regime unwillingness to negotiate

groundTruth.headline: US-Iran strike cycle active with multiple targets hit; Iranian military command centers, maritime capabilities, missile/drone sites, air defense systems degraded across Chabahar, Shiraz, Bandar Lengeh, and other sites
groundTruth.summary: CENTCOM completed tenth consecutive night of strikes on Iran by Jul 20 targeting military infrastructure. Intelligence Community assesses strikes unlikely to shift Iranian negotiating position. Escalation risk remains high with Iranian ballistic missile and drone responses documented.
groundTruth.signal: Sustained conflict with no off-ramp visible; US committed to degradation strategy; Iranian regime appears willing to absorb strikes and maintain aggressive posture toward Gulf partners and shipping

groundTruth.headline: Kuwait infrastructure under direct Iranian attack; multiple power plants, desalination facilities hit; Kuwait air defenses engaging incoming Iranian missiles and drones
groundTruth.summary: Fourth reported Iranian attack on Kuwaiti critical infrastructure in recent period with fires and severe damage. Kuwaiti air defenses actively intercepting hostile fire. US has established Camp Arifjan presence under threat.
groundTruth.signal: Iran escalating against US-aligned Gulf state infrastructure; direct threat to US military footprint at Camp Arifjan; energy supply disruption risk for regional allies; potential for civilian casualties and regional instability

groundTruth.headline: Multiple vessel strikes reported in Strait of Hormuz and Gulf of Oman by unknown projectiles; Kuwaiti-owned tanker Kaifan struck with crew abandonment; multiple UKMTO alerts issued
groundTruth.summary: Confirmed attacks on at least two vessels (Kaifan and another struck near Oman). Unknown attribution but occurs amid Iranian military operations. Vessel damage and crew abandonment indicate real maritime risk, not warning shots.
groundTruth.signal: Global energy security threat; oil price volatility risk; insurance and shipping costs rising; Houthis and Iranian proxies demonstrating ability to strike commercial traffic; potential for accidental escalation or widened targeting

groundTruth.headline: American death toll from Iran conflict at 17; dignified transfer ceremony at Dover Air Force Base postponed from Tuesday to Wednesday; Pentagon reported out of money
groundTruth.summary: Confirmed US servicemember casualties from recent Iran hostilities. Ceremony scheduling delays suggest logistics or identification issues. Pentagon budget constraint headline indicates potential readiness strain.
groundTruth.signal: Domestic political pressure building on administration; casualty numbers will drive Congressional scrutiny and media coverage; budget constraints may limit sustained operations tempo; force morale implications if personnel/logistics support degraded

groundTruth.headline: Gasoline prices back above $4 per gallon; Trump's campaign promise to cut electricity prices in half unfulfilled with rates up 18% in first months of term
groundTruth.summary: Energy price pressures contradicting administration messaging. Electricity rates rose 18% despite stated goal of 50% reduction over 18 months. Gas spike tied to Middle East disruption risk.
groundTruth.signal: Domestic political vulnerability on inflation/cost-of-living; Middle East instability feeding commodity prices; policy credibility erosion; potential for public opposition to continued military engagement if economic pain persists
